channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Banco Falabella corta integración tras incidente de proveedor en Colombia
item.description: Cibercriminales han puesto el foco en proveedores externos para monetizar ataques y golpear la reputación de la banca en Colombia, lo que llevó a Banco Falabella a desconectar este fin de semana su integración con un socio de cobranzas. “La respuesta del banco ha sido inmediata: se desactivaron los puntos de contacto con el proveedor y se reforzó la política de seguridad para exigir altos estándares en la protección de la información”, señaló la entidad a iupana . Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: After 62 Years, Florida Appeals Court Drops the Expert Witness Rule on Attorney Fees
item.description: Just over three years ago, Florida lawmakers effectively ended one-way attorney fees, disincentivizing much of the costly property insurance claims litigation that had plagued the industry. The 6 th District Court of Appeals, covering part of south-central Florida, threw out the 62-year-old court practice that had required expert witnesses and court hearings,. “I often wondered why we needed experts to tell judges (most of whom have practiced for many years) how to rule on attorney’s fees and costs,” said Lynn Brauer, a Miami plaintiffs’ attorney.

item.title: Ex-Deutsche Bank Manager Sues Bank for at Least $624 Million
item.description: A former Deutsche Bank AG manager who says he was wrongfully blamed for the Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA accounting scandal has sued the German lender in a London court for at least £473 million ($624 million) in damages. Michele Faissola, the former head of Deutsche Bank’s asset and wealth management business, as well as other ex-employees are suing the bank for at least £664 million in total, according to the filing seen by Bloomberg. Dario Schiraldi, a former manager in Deutsche Bank’s asset and wealth management division, has also filed a lawsuit in Frankfurt claiming around €152 million ($174 million) in damages.

item.title: JC Flowers acquires controlling stake in India’s Niyam Group
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Indian regulator approves shift to new financial reporting for insurers from Wednesday
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ‘The onus is on all of us’: Hong Kong’s ILS market needs buy in from local investors, Peak Re says
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aegon puts forward Marco Keim as nominee to the Supervisory Board a.s.r.
item.description: Aegon, a provider of life insurance, pensions and asset management services, has put forward Marco Keim as its nominee to the Supervisory Board of a.s.r. Should the appointment receive backing, Keim would join the Supervisory Board immediately after the meeting and serve until early July 2028, in line with the existing agreement governing the relationship between the two companies. The appointment would see Keim take over from Lard Friese as a non-independent member, reflecting Aegon’s position as a significant shareholder with an interest of roughly 24% in the Dutch insurer.

item.title: New Willis facility targets limited US casualty market umbrella capacity
item.description: Insurance broker Willis, a WTW business, has launched the Willis Excess Liability Lineslip (WELL) facility, an umbrella insurance solution designed to address limited capacity for large and complex casualty risks in the United States. casualty market where securing meaningful umbrella coverage has become increasingly difficult,” said James Sallada, Head of Casualty, North America, Willis. Providing up to $50 million of combined lead umbrella and first excess, the WELL facility offers organisations an additional option for securing higher liability limits, particularly as insurers continue to restrict umbrella capacity due to increased claim frequency and severity, explains the firm.

item.title: New Online Portal Will Allow a Deeper Dive Into Rural Tennessee
item.description: A new online dashboard will allow policymakers in Tennessee to move beyond superficial data and take a deep dive into why certain rural counties struggle, experts at East Tennessee State University said. The Tennessee Livability Indicators Dashboard was developed by ETSU’s Center for Rural Health and Research (CRHR), using data collected from various agencies about 60 topics related to the counties’ quality of life. Of Tennessee’s 95 counties, 78 are designated as rural, and 70 counties have more than half of their residents in rural areas.

item.title: Hacked Hospitals, Hidden Spyware: Iran Conflict Shows Digital Fight in Warfare
item.description: “The fact it’s synced and at the same minute … is a first.” The digital fight is likely to persist even if a ceasefire is reached, experts said, because it’s a lot easier and cheaper than conventional conflict and because it is designed not to kill or conquer, but to spy, steal and frighten. The operation, attributed to Iran, showed sophisticated coordination and is just the latest tactic in a cyber conflict that pits the U.S. and Israel against Iran and its digital proxies.

item.title: More Insurance M&A Deals on the Horizon?
item.description: • Falling interest rates and a “more strategic approach” to acquisitions drove a stabilization of M&A activity during 2025, according to Clyde & Co’s annual “Insurance Growth Update,” which tallied 211 carrier and broker deals globally in 2025, up from just over 200 in 2024. and Bermuda, Deloitte’s “ 2026 Insurance M&A Outlook: Eye on the Ball ,” counted a lot more 2025 deals—455—including 411 broker deals, 26 P/C carrier deals and 11 in the life and annuity insurance segment. Two firms tracking mergers and acquisitions in the insurance industry reported changes in property/casualty insurance deals last year, with one describing “increased momentum” and another “stabilization.” Both, however, indicated that selectivity and strategic approaches characterized 2025 deals—a dynamic they believe is likely to continue in 2026.

item.title: INSTANDA Launches MAX for AI-Driven Commercial Underwriting
item.description: INSTANDA (London) has launched INSTANDA MAX , an underwriting capability designed to support large-scale commercial lines policies with item-level data and real-time processing.The solution enables insurers to underwrite thousands of assets within a single policy while applying granular risk assessment and pricing at the individual item level. “Commercial line insurers have traditionally been forced to underwrite large, complex asset classes using aggregated data and broad assumptions, because the operational burden of doing otherwise was simply too high,” says Tim Hardcastle, CEO, INSTANDA. For the first time, commercial line insurers can process bulk policies containing thousands of assets while still applying granular, measurable data to each individual item, supported by strong data governance, human oversight, ecosystem integration and frictionless operational AI.” The platform is designed to address limitations in traditional underwriting approaches, where aggregate-level analysis can lead to pricing inaccuracies and delayed adjustments.

item.title: Willis Launches Facility to Expand U.S. Umbrella Capacity
item.description: Willis (London) has launched the Willis Excess Liability Lineslip facility to address limited umbrella capacity in the U.S. “With the WELL facility, we are bringing together global capacity to provide organizations with access to higher limits, streamlined placement and tailored solutions—helping them protect against catastrophic exposures and confidently manage complex risks.” The solution offers up to $50 million in combined lead umbrella and first excess capacity, including up to $25 million in lead umbrella coverage and an additional $25 million in first excess. Developed by Willis’ North America Casualty team in London, the facility leverages the London market and a consortium of Lloyd’s syndicates to provide capacity through a single policy structure.

item.title: MCP as the Control Plane for AI Tooling in Insurance
item.description: Slipping through the cracks is not a desirable trait, and MCP gives teams a control plane for necessary transactions and interactions. Agentic AI does not become useful in an insurance organization when someone writes a clever prompt. Most teams still treat “tooling” for agentic AI as a string of one-off integrations held together by spit, sweat, and hope.

item.title: Weeks-Long Australian LNG Outage Will Further Tighten Supply
item.description: A weeks-long outage at a major Australian liquefied natural gas export plant has dealt a further blow to a global market still reeling from the halt of operations in Qatar. Storm damage to Chevron Corp.’s Wheatstone gas plant is hampering efforts to restart operations and the facility won’t be back online fully for “a number of weeks,” the company said Sunday. said Sunday it’s working to resume normal operations at its North West Shelf facility, while Chevron said its Gorgon site was operating at “full rates” after an outage at one of its three production units.

item.title: BofA to Pay $72.5 Million to Settle Epstein Victim Lawsuit
item.description: agreed to pay $72.5 million to settle a lawsuit on behalf of Jeffrey Epstein victims claiming the bank aided in the late financier’s sex-trafficking. Related: BofA Agrees to Settle Claims It Aided Epstein Sex Crimes “While we stand by our prior statements made in the filings in this case, including that Bank of America did not facilitate sex trafficking crimes, this resolution allows us to put this matter behind us and provides further closure for the plaintiffs,” bank spokesman Bill Halldin said in an emailed statement. co-founder Leon Black used Bank of America accounts to transfer $170 million to Epstein.

item.title: LaGuardia Crash Bolsters Case for Using AI in Air Control Towers
item.description: Inside a mock control tower next to Hong Kong International Airport, a virtual near-miss plays out on multi-panel screens. The simulation bears an uncanny and coincidental resemblance to a tragedy that unfolded at New York City’s LaGuardia Airport, where an Air Canada Express jet with 76 people on board smashed into a fire truck shortly after landing on March 22. The force of that crash obliterated the cockpit, killing the two pilots and demolishing the emergency services vehicle trying to cross the runway.

item.title: Electric Bikes Pose Safety, Injury Risks
item.description: E-bikes offer a convenient, eco-friendly, and cost-effective alternative to cars, but their increasing use is drawing safety concerns. The 14-year-old was riding an electric bicycle at an estimated 25 mph when he slammed into Janet Stotko during her evening walk, leaving her unconscious and bleeding on a sidewalk in her Minnesota neighborhood. At a checkup, she said, her doctors told her, “Wow, we can’t believe you’re here.” Now, she’s pushing for stricter laws regulating e-bikes in hopes that others won’t be hurt.

item.title: Report Reveals Mixed Collision Trends as Fleets Work to Implement Safety Programs
item.description: A new report indicates that collision rates are beginning to level off, rising just 4% in 2025, according to analysis by fleet telematics provider Lytx. “The 2026 Road Safety Report spotlights not only the significant victories we’ve secured but also the formidable risks that persist,” said Chris Cabrera, Chief Executive Officer at Lytx. The collision rate in trucking rose just 2% in 2025, down from a 19% increase in 2024, as demand for freight transportation cooled.

item.title: Progressive expands aggregate reinsurance cover, renews shared hurricane limit for 2026
item.description: US insurer Progressive expanded its aggregate excess-of-loss reinsurance protection at the start of 2026 and has also renewed a hurricane season focused shared reinsurance limit arrangement to cover losses from any storms this year. In addition, Progressive has already begun making reinsurance preparations for the 2026 hurricane season , having already renewed a shared limit arrangement. Progressive’s $300 million of aggregate reinsurance limit for 2026 has retention thresholds of $550 million and $750 million.

item.title: P&C profitability nears peak amid rising competition and strong balance sheets, says Moody’s
item.description: This analysis from Moody’s underlines that while P&C profitability remains strong, competitive pressures and selective risk exposures may moderate growth, even as balance sheets are reinforced across the sector. According to Moody’s Ratings, a provider of credit ratings and risk analysis, profitability in the property and casualty (P&C) reinsurance sector appears to have reached its peak as competition intensifies across all lines of business. Moody’s emphasises that despite these strategic adjustments, solvency ratios remain robust, with the largest reinsurers continuing to strengthen their balance sheets through enhanced reserves.

item.title: Michigan School District Settles Suit Over Pledge of Allegiance Dispute
item.description: A suburban Detroit school district has agreed to give First Amendment training to staff to settle a lawsuit by a teenager who said a teacher humiliated her for refusing to stand for the Pledge of Allegiance in protest of U.S. Danielle, whose family is of Palestinian descent, declined to recite the pledge at her school over three days in January 2025. The school district will remove anything from Danielle’s file that suggests her actions violated school policy, according to the settlement.

item.title: Texas Pizza Hut Franchise to Pay $35K in Harassment Suit
item.description: Ayvaz Pizza, LLC, doing business as Pizza Hut, which operates more than 350 Pizza Hut restaurants around the country, including multiple locations in the Houston area, will pay $35,000 and furnish other relief to settle a sex harassment and retaliation lawsuit filed by the U.S. According to the EEOC’s lawsuit, a former female assistant manager of a Pizza Hut restaurant in Porter, Texas was subjected to a hostile work environment by her supervisor after she broke off their romantic relationship. In addition to providing $35,000 in damages to the aggrieved worker, the two-year consent decree signed March 19 requires Ayvaz Pizza to provide the worker a letter of reference; update and disseminate its anti-discrimination policies; provide comprehensive anti-discrimination training to its staff; and report sex-based discrimination and harassment complaints to the EEOC.

item.title: Taylor Swift Sued for Trademark Infringement Over ‘Life of a Showgirl’
item.description: Pop megastar Taylor Swift was sued by a Las Vegas performer on Monday who said Swift’s latest hit album “The Life of a Showgirl” violates her trademark rights. Wade said in the complaint that Swift’s continued use of the “Life of a Showgirl” name “drowns out” her trademark “until consumers begin to assume that the original is the imitation.” “What Plaintiff had built over twelve years, Defendants threatened to swallow in weeks,” Wade said. Wade’s attorney Jaymie Parkkinen said they “have great respect for Swift’s talent and success, but trademark law exists to ensure that creators at all levels can protect what they’ve built.” “The Life of a Showgirl,” Swift’s 12th album, was released in O c tober and shattered Spotify streaming records on its way to becoming the biggest-selling album of 2025.

item.title: Carriers Lose Millions on Manual Claims
item.description: Insurers hemorrhage millions on manual claims processing; targeted automation slashes costs by half within months. The reality is that most initiatives fail because firms try to "boil the ocean." I've seen carriers sink $2 million and 18 months into building a "total automation" platform, only to find it handles 30% of their claim types while the rest still require a manual "workaround." The project is branded a failure, and the organization develops an allergy to the word "automation" for the next three years. From what I've seen across the industry, carriers are burning between $7 and $15 just to manually process a single claim document.

item.title: SelectQuote receives NYSE notice over stock price
item.description: SelectQuote Insurance Services said it received a notice from the New York Stock Exchange after its shares traded below $1.00 on average over 30 consecutive trading days, putting it out of compliance with listing standards. The company has a six month cure period to regain compliance and said it plans to monitor its stock price and take necessary actions. Shares will continue trading on the NYSE during this period.

item.title: Aon backs eMed
item.description: eMed has raised $200 million at a valuation exceeding $2 billion, positioning itself as a platform focused on employer-sponsored population health, particularly around GLP-1 and GIP therapies. eMed offers clinically supervised weight management programs centered on GLP-1 medications, targeting employers looking to offer these benefits at scale. The round was led by Aon Consulting , with participation from a mix of institutional and strategic investors, including Tom Brady , who also serves as founding chief wellness officer, alongside investors tied to healthcare, private equity, and technology.

item.title: SIMS raises equity for disability income product targeting youth athletes
item.description: Specialty Insurance Marketing Services is positioning its ScholarGuard product as a niche accident disability offering designed to protect future education costs for youth athletes. It is distributed through licensed life and health insurers, with SIMS acting as the marketing and product development entity. Separately, SIMS has raised capital through a private offering, targeting up to $3 million, with approximately $520,000 sold to date .

item.title: U.S. Treasury to Consult With Insurance Regulators on Private Credit Lenders
item.description: Treasury Department is expected to convene in the coming weeks the first of a series of meetings with domestic and international insurance regulators about recent developments in jittery private credit markets, two sources familiar with the plans told Reuters. During remarks to the Economic Club of Dallas in February, Bessent, a former hedge fund manager, said that when assets move from private credit lenders into regulated financial institutions, such as pension funds, banks, or captive insurance companies, “Treasury gets involved.” “I am concerned with watching, how does this get to the regulated financial system,” Bessent said. Based on the results of that meeting, the participants will determine the direction of future engagements, aiming to improve regulators’ fact-based, transparent oversight of private credit lenders as their interactions with regulated financial institutions increase.

item.title: Jio Financial Services targets 2026 entry into life, general insurance
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Shojin Financial Services Limited enters administration
item.description: Shojin Financial Services Limited (Shojin) is a crowdfunding platform authorised and regulated by the FCA. On 23 March 2026, Shojin went into administration. If you are affected by Shojin entering administration, the Joint Administrators will get in touch with you directly.

item.title: FCA confirms motor finance redress scheme
item.description: We are going ahead with a scheme to compensate motor finance customers who were treated unfairly. We want to provide certainty for consumers and finality for firms and investors, supporting the ongoing availability of competitively priced motor finance. An industry-wide scheme is the quickest and most cost effective way to deliver fair compensation.

item.title: Millions of car finance customers to get payouts this year as FCA goes ahead with compensation scheme
item.description: Millions of motor finance customers will receive compensation this year under an FCA scheme for those treated unfairly by firms who broke the law by failing to disclose important information. Delivering compensation promptly also gives lenders the chance to rebuild trust, and means we can draw a line under the past and support a healthy motor finance market for the future.' An industry-wide scheme is the most efficient way of compensating affected consumers while supporting the ongoing availability of competitively priced motor finance for millions who rely on it. 'Now we need everyone to get behind it and ensure millions get their money this year.

item.title: Zenkyoren targets mid-guidance pricing for $100m Nakama Re 2026-1 Japan quake cat bond
item.description: Zenkyoren is now targeting mid-guidance pricing for the $100 million of multi-year collateralized Japanese earthquake reinsurance protection it aims to secure from its new Nakama Re Pte. This new Nakama Re 2026-1 issuance will mark the sixteenth catastrophe bond directly sponsored by Zenkyoren that we have listed in our extensive Deal Directory . The Nakama Re Series 2026-1 Class 1 cat bond notes will also have an initial annualised attachment probability of 0.87%, an initial annualised expected loss of 0.82%, and were initially offered to cat bond investors with price guidance for a risk interest spread in a range from 1.9% to 2.4%.

item.title: El Niño to shape 2026 Atlantic hurricane season. US landfall threat remains: Accuweather
item.description: The 2026 Atlantic hurricane season will be shaped by shifting climate patterns in the Pacific, as a developing El Niño may influence and perhaps depress tropical storm formation, but a forecast from Accuweather highlights the potential for rapid intensification and warns of three to five direct storm impacts on the United States. Waters are already warm in the Atlantic, Caribbean and Gulf in regions where early tropical storm formation has tended to occur, which leads Accuweather to forecast a chance of an earlier start to the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season. “When storms develop so close to land, it means there is less time for coastal residents to prepare for potential impacts.” But, El Niño conditions are expected to be the main influence to the overall season in terms of hurricane and storm formation, meaning storm numbers may be around or even below historical averages, Accuweather explains.

item.title: LA wildfires a real-world stress test. Investor confidence in wildfire cat bonds rose: Swiss Re
item.description: The devastating Palisades and Eaton wildfires that struck the Los Angeles area in January 2025 served as a real-world stress test for outstanding wildfire catastrophe bond structures, while also increasing investor confidence in allocating to the wildfire peril, according to Swiss Re Capital Markets. Investor appetite for wildfire risk appeared driven by a confluence of factors: improved wildfire modelling, refined contracts and structures.” Swiss Re Capital Markets outlined that both the Palisades and Eaton wildfires have not triggered outstanding U.S. “Somewhat counterintuitively, both wildfire events significantly increased investor confidence into wildfire cat bonds.

item.title: The Hartford strengthens international business with leadership updates
item.description: US-based insurer The Hartford has made several leadership updates within its international business as it continues to strengthen capabilities for long-term, sustainable growth. Samuel joined The Hartford in January 2026 as International Head of Marine, Energy & Industrial Risk. He has spent nearly seven years at The Hartford, most recently serving as Head of Casualty, International.

item.title: Howden Re boosts Latin America and Caribbean offering with senior hires
item.description: Howden Re, the global reinsurance, capital markets and strategic advisory arm of Howden Group, has appointed Carlos Garcia as Managing Director and Karina Lopez as Director, further strengthening its Latin America and Caribbean offering. April McLaughlin, Head of Howden Miami, commented, “Latin America and the Caribbean is a key growth region for Howden Re, presenting a compelling opportunity: a large, complex and underserved market where clients are increasingly looking for brokers with genuine local expertise and access to global capacity. Carlos and Karina’s depth of experience across the region’s most exposed markets, combined with their technical rigour and strong client relationships, further strengthens the proposition we are building for clients across Latin America and the Caribbean.” Mario Baotic, Head of International Growth Markets, International Treaty, Howden Re, added, “This is a region with significant catastrophe exposure, a rapidly maturing insurance market and real appetite for specialist reinsurance solutions.

item.title: Commodity prices rise due to Middle East conflict: Coface
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East is causing commodity prices to soar, driven by disruptions to the supply of raw materials via the Strait of Hormuz. “The current escalation in the Middle East is hitting commodity markets hard. According to a recent FLASH NEWS report by Coface, the conflict has triggered a “deadlock” scenario that is significantly impacting the oil & gas, fertilizers, petrochemical derivatives and aluminium markets.

item.title: Hawaii to Take Over Aging Dam After Failure Scare During Floods
item.description: A 120-year-old Hawaii dam that reached worrisome levels during heavy rains and devastating flooding, prompting thousands of residents to evacuate for fear of life-threatening failure last week, will soon be taken over by the state. The state’s land board on Friday voted to acquire certain irrigation lands from Dole Food Co., clearing the way for the state to take over the aging dam and move forward on at least $20 million in repairs and an expansion of the spillway. Related: The Scope of Damage From Hawaii’s Floods Becomes Clearer The earthen structure was built in 1906 to increase sugar production for the Waialua Agricultural Co., which eventually became a subsidiary of Dole Food Co.

item.title: Sources: US Treasury to Consult With Insurance Regulators on Private Credit Lenders
item.description: Treasury Department is expected to convene in coming weeks the first of a series of meetings with domestic and international insurance regulators about recent developments in jittery private credit markets, two sources familiar with the plans told Reuters. Treasury officials are keen to hear regulators’ feedback on the rising use of fund-level leverage, the consistency of private credit ratings, the use of offshore reinsurance, and the liquidity of investments in private credit markets, the sources said, adding that any policy prescriptions would only come after a series of consultations. During remarks to the Economic Club of Dallas in February, Bessent, a former hedge fund manager, said that when assets move from private credit lenders into regulated financial institutions, such as pension funds, banks or captive insurance companies, “Treasury gets involved.” “I am concerned with watching, how does this get to the regulated financial system,” Bessent said.

item.title: Missing Crew Not Found in Iran-Hit Vessel, Thai Ship Owner Says
item.description: A search for three crew members reported missing after a Thai-flagged cargo vessel was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz earlier this month failed to locate them, according to ship’s owner. The ship ran aground on an Iranian island last week, and a search team that boarded the vessel couldn’t find the missing crew, Precious Shipping Managing Director Khalid M. Of the 23 crew members on board, 20 were rescued by the Omani navy after abandoning the vessel in a lifeboat.

item.title: Prudential Financial appoints Joseph Barrowclough President of Bermuda Reinsurance to drive platform growth
item.description: The post Prudential Financial appoints Joseph Barrowclough President of Bermuda Reinsurance to drive platform growth appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Prudential Financial has appointed Joseph Barrowclough as President of its Bermuda Reinsurance operations, reinforcing its leadership team as it continues to expand its global reinsurance capabilities. Commenting on his new position, Barrowclough highlighted the opportunity to further enhance Prudential’s Bermuda platforms and drive continued growth, supported by a strong team and a clear strategic vision.

item.title: WTW strengthens EMEA structure with new leadership to accelerate growth and AI adoption
item.description: The post WTW strengthens EMEA structure with new leadership to accelerate growth and AI adoption appeared first on Insurtech Insights . WTW has introduced a refreshed leadership structure across its EMEA Insurance Consulting and Technology business, aimed at enhancing specialisation, accelerating innovation, and supporting growing client demand for technology-driven solutions. The changes position the business to further strengthen its role as a strategic partner to insurers across EMEA, particularly as the industry accelerates digital transformation and AI adoption.

item.title: Odyssey Group Holdings unifies Hudson and Newline under single brand to strengthen global identity
item.description: The post Odyssey Group Holdings unifies Hudson and Newline under single brand to strengthen global identity appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Odyssey Group Holdings has brought its subsidiaries Hudson Insurance and Newline Group under a unified brand identity, aligning all operations under the group’s long-standing wave symbol. By aligning Hudson, Newline, and OdysseyRe under one identity, the group aims to highlight its collective financial strength, underwriting expertise, and long-term stability.

item.title: MSIG Asia invests in Ancileo
item.description: MSIG Asia announced a strategic equity investment and regional partnership with travel insurance technology provider Ancileo to accelerate its travel insurance growth across Asia. By combining with Ancileo’s technology and distribution expertise, we will deliver faster, more relevant experiences for travellers and create long-term value for our partners and customers.” – Clemens Philippi, CEO of MSIG Asia. “MSIG has been a valued partner of Ancileo for years, and today’s announcement reflects what we believe a true long-term partnership can become.

item.title: Just selects Sapiens platform for UK retirement modernization
item.description: Sapiens announced that Just Group has selected its insurance platform for life and pensions to support system modernization and growth in the UK retirement market. The platform will support Just’s ability to scale operations and expand its product portfolio across retirement offerings. Strategically, the move reflects Just’s focus on building a more flexible and data-driven infrastructure to handle growing demand in the retirement segment.

item.title: IA Valuations Announces Strategic Partnership with HawkSoft
item.description: This partnership will benefit agencies using HawkSoft by giving them a trusted outlet for insurance agency valuations and agency business planning. “Our partnership with IA Valuations changes that by connecting HawkSoft’s operational data with IA Valuations’ expertise. Partnership connects performance analytics with HawkSoft’s agency management system to deliver insights that help owners confidently plan for their future.

item.title: El futuro del seguro se juega en la gestión del talento
item.description: El sector asegurador afronta uno de los mayores desafíos de su historia en materia de gestión del talento, impulsado por la automatización, el riesgo climático y el cambio en las expectativas de la fuerza laboral. Así lo señala un reciente informe de Aon , que sitúa la construcción de una estrategia de talento preparada para el futuro como una prioridad crítica para las aseguradoras . El rol incluye la gestión de proveedores, supervisión de mantenimientos y apoyo logístico interno para garantizar un entorno de trabajo eficiente y seguro.

item.title: Del nuevo registro de VPL al deber de información: el sector asegurador se adapta a los cambios legales
item.description: También destacó el nuevo registro de los VPL según el RD 52/2026. A continuación, José Luis Quilis, responsable de suscripción y siniestros de CGPA , trajo a colación el deber de información de los Mediadores de Seguros subrayando los principios generales de honestidad, imparcialidad y equidad, dando publicidad precisa, clara y no engañosa y evitar conflicto te intereses a la hora de tomar decisiones vía remuneración, que se recogen en el Real Decreto-ley 3/2020 , que “ha supuesto un punto de inflexión en el deber de asesoramiento de los distribuidores de seguros”, concluyó Quilis. Roberto Hernández, director de siniestros de Zurich y Francisco Lisbona, director de Zurich Resilience Solutions , expusieron los retos para el sector asegurador tras la DANA .
